entities.name: Cisco
entities.how_described: 本集将其描述为全球 AI 基础设施建设中最核心的玩家之一。不仅在大公司内部完成了自上而下的 AI 优先转型,还通过提供网络、光学、安全和数据平台技术,将不同厂商制造的 GPU 连接起来形成集群,是 AI 时代的关键基础设施公司。

evidence.quote: To turn Cisco from an older, slower, more traditional enterprise to a very AI-forward company, this is very difficult to do.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Today, my guest is Jeetu Patel, chief product officer and president at Cisco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Cisco is not a brand that mostly people think about when they think about AI, but not only are they a massive part of the AI infrastructure build-out that is happening right now all over the world.

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And the way he described it is the work that you have done to turn Cisco the way he described it from an older, slower, more traditional enterprise to a very AI-forward company.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: What are two or three things that you've done that you think have been most impactful and effective in helping Cisco lean into AI, not be scared of it, and actually embrace the future?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so, we made that choice that says, Cisco is going to be not just an iconic company.

evidence.quote: He's like, "I want Cisco not just to be an iconic company.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I want Cisco to also be an iconic and innovative company."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The way that individual success was defined was everyone wanted to be a GM at Cisco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But you don't have to buy everything all at once, because we also want to be realistic about the fact that not every customer only uses Cisco top to bottom.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I want to take a tangent and make sure people understand what Cisco even does these days.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I think as a lay person, you'll think about Cisco and you're like, "Okay, they WebEx, yes.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Give us just a quick glimpse into how Cisco fits into this massive build-out and just what does Cisco these days?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Cisco is a critical infrastructure company for the AI era.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And the third category of machine data we can play a massive role in at Cisco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So, what does Cisco do then?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And that's what Cisco does.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Is it going to be logical for people that Cisco built it versus another company building it?"
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so, I think in order to stop that, what you have to do is you have to actually use your scale as an advantage and you have to use the areas where you've got the ability to have permission to play where people feel like this is very logical for a company like Cisco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I'm curious if there's an example you can share either from Box or Cisco where it's just like, "Okay, this is like we're going to do this because we have the permission to play here."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Example of permission to play is, I mean, there's so many ideas that a company the size of Cisco, we have constantly new ideas that keep coming up.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so, why are we not in business-to-consumer tech at Cisco?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: That's an area where it would be extremely hard for people to growth that Cisco should be the one who's participating in that.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And by the way, he's the CEO of Cisco, in case people-
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: He's the CEO of Cisco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So, you're currently CPO at Cisco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I don't know if it was ... I mean, I instinctively kind of knew it, but it was very, very accentuated at Cisco, because when people say, "Oh, is scale hard?"

evidence.quote: What I found over the years, not just at Cisco, but even when I ... Because I ran a small startup in Chicago for 17 years before I moved over to the Valley, what I found in the large companies is, the communication framework and the lossiness of communication, the telephone game, so to say, has a profoundly negative effect if you're not intentional about it and if you're not careful of it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And that is probably one of the lessons that I learned at Cisco in a very stark way, which I didn't fully appreciate it until I got into the details of the infrastructure going, "Wow, if this thing doesn't work," we were ... Every single time, our infrastructure doesn't work.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So I actually feel like when I got this new job to run all product for Cisco, there's zero chance I would've been able to do it if AI wasn't there.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: NVIDIA
entities.how_described: 本集提到它是制造 AI 算力核心组件 GPU 的厂商,由 Jensen 领导,在 AI 深度思考和发展方向上正在做着非凡的事情。Cisco 则负责将他们制造的 GPU 连接在一起形成网络。

evidence.quote: That's what we end up doing is NVIDIA makes the GPUs and we connect those GPUs together.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Box
entities.how_described: 本集提到这是嘉宾的前东家,他在这里工作时与前老板 Aaron Levie 探讨了产品战略中的「获胜权」和「参与许可」概念。

evidence.quote: I talked to Aaron Levie, your former boss at Box.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Is it going to be logical for people that Box built it versus another company building it?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I mean, it's cliche, but I feel like what ChatGPT, Gemini and Claude have done, it's changed lives.

entities.name: 主观能动性 (agency)
entities.how_described: 本集提到 CEO 雇佣了嘉宾并给予他去执行内部变革的自主权与能动性(agency),强调了高层授权在大型企业转型中的重要性。

evidence.quote: A lot of the movements that we've had internally wouldn't have happened if he had not hired me and given me agency to go do the things that I needed to get done.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: AI 优先 (AI-first)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成是 Cisco 自上而下确定的不可动摇的战略决心。嘉宾明确告诉员工:如果不灵活掌握 AI,长远来看工作就不保。这是大公司转身第一步要明确「不容讨论」的事项。

evidence.quote: What Jeetu has achieved internally at Cisco in terms of transforming their culture and ways of working to be AI-first is something that most big company leaders only dream about.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: The entire company is on the same page, we are an AI-first company.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then not being confused about the fact that we'll be AI-first from the top down.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 能力过剩 (capabilities overhang)
entities.how_described: 本集提到这是 AI 峰会的核心洞察之一:科技界用科学解决惊人难题的能力供给,远超目前企业实际应用和落地的能力,形成了所谓的「进步的悖论」。

evidence.quote: One is the capabilities overhang is real.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So, that was one is, the capabilities overhang is real.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 超级趋势 (megatrend)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成是人类历史中最基础、不可阻挡的大趋势(如 AI)。给出了一个极佳的辨别启发式:如果必须要拿个博士学位才能听懂某技术能为大众做什么,它大概率只是炒作周期,而不是超级趋势。

evidence.quote: You have to know the difference between a megatrend and a hype cycle.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: When there's a megatrend, don't fight it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: AI is a megatrend, one of the most foundational movements that we have seen in human history.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And by the way, you have to know the difference between a megatrend and a hype cycle.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: When there's a megatrend, don't fight it and don't succumb to the temptation of trying to go out and do vanity work for a hype cycle.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I imagine AI megatrend.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: AI is a megatrend in my mind, and there's a bunch of hype cycles we've had where I never particularly subscribe to them.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: When you feel like you need a PhD to understand what someone's saying, chances are it ain't going to be a megatrend, because by definition, a megatrend is it's going to impact a large population of the world.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 炒作周期 (hype cycle)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成是与「超级趋势」相对立的虚荣概念(如 Web3)。特征是过度复杂、难以向普通人解释其实际用途,不值得企业去盲目跟风对抗真正的趋势。

evidence.quote: You have to know the difference between a megatrend and a hype cycle.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And by the way, you have to know the difference between a megatrend and a hype cycle.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: When there's a megatrend, don't fight it and don't succumb to the temptation of trying to go out and do vanity work for a hype cycle.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 获胜权 (right to win)
entities.how_described: 本集提到它是检验公司是否该进入某市场的战略标准:不仅要能造出产品,还要有合理的分销渠道和「参与许可」,让市场和客户觉得你做这件事是合乎逻辑的。

evidence.quote: And he share this concept of the right to win, which he says has informed the way he thinks about strategy ever since.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So, that's this notion of permission to play, the right to win.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Do we have a right to win in that area because we have permission to play?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 参与许可 (permission to play)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成是评估业务边界的关键判断标准。例如，Cisco 需要确保其进入市场的切入点合乎逻辑，并拥有将产品实现大规模分销的渠道与优势（如利用其在网络基础设施领域的既有规模与信任），才能获得参与许可；反之，若缺乏这些条件，就会导致产品与销售团队相互指责并浪费资金。

evidence.quote: One of the things that we would always talk about is in the areas that we are going to participate, do we have permission to play?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Every company has to make sure that the way in which they provide points of insertion and logical entry into a market is a lot of times dependent on, do you have the permission to play in that market?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So, that's this notion of permission to play, the right to win.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Do we have a right to win in that area because we have permission to play?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so, I think in order to stop that, what you have to do is you have to actually use your scale as an advantage and you have to use the areas where you've got the ability to have permission to play where people feel like this is very logical for a company like Cisco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I'm curious if there's an example you can share either from Box or Cisco where it's just like, "Okay, this is like we're going to do this because we have the permission to play here."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Example of permission to play is, I mean, there's so many ideas that a company the size of Cisco, we have constantly new ideas that keep coming up.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I don't think that we've got permission to play there.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 丢包 (packet loss)
entities.how_described: 本集借用这个网络术语来比喻大企业层级中的信息流失:如果一号位不亲自到一线去讲愿景和核心故事,而是像玩「传话游戏」那样层层下达,信息一定会被加料或损耗。

evidence.quote: We have this concept in networking called packet loss.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: You don't want to have packet loss in your storytelling from you to the person on the front line because-
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: This is just a direct connection and there's no packet loss of this one.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 松散耦合 (loosely coupled)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成是 Cisco 向「平台公司」转型时的核心系统架构特征:各产品之间依赖少、独立解耦,但组合使用时又能紧密集成产生奇效,避免了内部各自为政的孤岛。

evidence.quote: So, loosely coupled, but tightly integrated.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 围墙花园 (walled garden)
entities.how_described: 本集提到这是 Cisco 转型时决定彻底打破的心态。公司不能再封闭运作,而必须融入开放的生态系统,甚至要习惯与竞争对手合作以促成客户的成功。

evidence.quote: This was a very deliberate decision, which was, we cannot operate in a walled garden.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 护栏 (guardrails)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成是 AI 系统在应用于社会和企业业务时必须设置的安全与制衡机制,因为 AI 自身带有不可预测的风险和非确定性的野心。

evidence.quote: And we have to make sure that we actually keep guardrails around that, because it is in service of humans, it is not to go out and build a society by itself.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And as you have more automation in the world, being intentional not just with humans but also with machines and what you want to do to create the guardrails, I think is pretty important.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 幻觉 (hallucination)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成是导致企业对大语言模型产生「信任赤字」的核心原因:写诗时它是特性,但在运行需要可预测性的关键业务系统时,它就成了破坏性的缺陷。

evidence.quote: Hallucination is a feature when you're writing poetry, but when you're trying to go out and run predictable systems, hallucination can be a bad thing.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0
